text: Calibrated probabilistic predictive models are a fundamental component of many AI systems. In this paper we introduce a new non-parametric calibration method called Bayesian Binning into Quantiles (BBQ). This method overcomes the current shortcomings of calibration methods. Our method can be trivially applied to the output of a classifier (post processing the output of a classifier), making it extensible to any current classification method. We demonstrate the efficacy of our method through a series of experiments which show that it is computationally efficient and highly accurate. These experiments were performed on a number of common UCI datasets as well as a number of simulated datasets.
